boundary: audio-only input + fixed task-definition text (label set / MCQ options / tool registry / JSON schema); no golden transcript/answer/intent ever placed in the prompt -- see templates.py module docstring's Information-Boundary-Guard note.

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Which of these factors increases the risk for lung cancer?
Options:
A. Saturated fat
B. Vitamin D deficiency
C. Lack of physical activity
D. High dose ß-carotene supplements
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
If the frequency of one source of sound is 500 Hertz and a second sound source is 504 Hertz, then when the two waves interfere with each other, the beat frequency will be
Options:
A. 10 Hertz
B. 4 Hertz
C. 1,004 Hertz
D. 504 Hertz
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
The price elasticity of demand for a product is greater if
Options:
A. the proportion of the good of the consumer's budget is high.
B. the number of substitute products is limited.
C. the product is unique and has no substitutes.
D. the consumer has a high income.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: D. the consumer has a high income.

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Which of the following is true regarding the complications of a large goitre?
Options:
A. Dysphagia is an indication for surgery
B. It causes severe headaches due to increased pressure in the neck area
C. It causes shortness of breath due to compression of the lungs
D. It increases the risk of thyroid cancer
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: D. It increases the risk of thyroid cancer

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.

Options:
A. $19.23
B. $30.00
C. $25.00
D. $26.00
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Which of the following is a formal region?
Options:
A. Dixie
B. The area influenced by a particular weather system
C. The Midwest
D. Pennsylvania
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
At the outset of group counseling, the therapist should explain char the obligation of the group member to respect confidentiality is
Options:
A. on the “honor system” only
B. protected by state/provincial laws
C. less of an issue than it is in individual therapy
D. mandated by federal law
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
The radius of Mars is about half that of Earth; the mass of Mars is about one-tenth that of Earth. Which of the following is closest to the gravitational field at the surface of Mars?
Options:
A. 0.5 Newtons per kilogram
B. 1 Newtons per kilogram
C. 2 Newtons per kilogram
D. 4 Newtons per kilogram
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Hume's attack on natural law is founded on his argument that:
Options:
A. We cannot objectively know what is morally right or wrong.
B. Natural law is backward-looking.
C. There is no such thing as a social contract.
D. Natural law fails to protect the state against attacks.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: A. We cannot objectively know what is morally right or wrong.

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
A car starts from rest and reaches a speed of 30 miles per hour in 8 seconds. What is its acceleration?
Options:
A. 6.5 feet per second per second
B. 6.0 feet per second per second
C. 5.5 feet per second per second
D. 4.0 feet per second per second
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: C. 5.5 feet per second per second

D. 384 pounds

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Under which conditions will a real gas most closely behave as an ideal gas?
Options:
A. Low volume and high pressure
B. Low temperature and low pressure
C. High temperature and low pressure
D. High volume and high pressure
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: B. Low temperature and low pressure

Options:
A. electrons; electrons.
B. nucleons; electrons.
C. electrons; nucleons.
D. electrons; protons.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

A change in a neuron membrane potential from +50 millivolts to -70 millivolts is considered
Options:
A. a synapse potential
B. hyperpolarization
C. a graded potential
D. repolarization
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Which of the following characteristics is predicted for an early-successional plant community?
Options:
A. High frequency of R-selected species
B. High diversity of plant species
C. High ratios of primary production to standing-crop biomass
D. Low rates of photosynthesis
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: A. High frequency of R-selected species

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Which of these principles is not an element of the responsibility to protect?
Options:
A. The responsibility to remain sovereign.
B. The responsibility to refuse.
C. The responsibility to recognize.
D. The responsibility to rebuild.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: A. The responsibility to remain sovereign.

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Which of the following is true of the spine?
Options:
A. Schober's test is designed to quantify the flexion of the lumbar spine
B. The lumbar spine is the upper part of the spine
C. The spine is incapable of healing after injury
D. The human spine has 20 vertebrae
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: A. Schober's test is designed to quantify the flexion of the lumbar spine

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
The appropriate fiscal policy to remedy a recession
Options:
A. calls for the federal government to run a deficit.
B. calls for the federal government to run a surplus.
C. is increased taxes and government spending.
D. is decreased government spending and taxes.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: D. is decreased government spending and taxes.

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
What is a tracheostomy?
Options:
A. An opening in the anterior chest wall.
B. An opening in the anterior wall of the trachea below the cricoid cartilage.
C. An opening in the anterior chest wall, above the diaphragm.
D. An opening in the posterior wall of the esophagus.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: B. An opening in the anterior wall of the trachea below the cricoid cartilage.

C. The complexity of the voting process
D. A large number of independent candidates

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
How does freedom of choice fit in the capitalist framework?
Options:
A. Capitalism restricts freedom of choice
B. Freedom of choice opposes the principles of capitalism
C. Freedom of choice is an essential feature of capitalism
D. Freedom of choice is irrelevant in capitalism
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: C. Freedom of choice is an essential feature of capitalism

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Gene flow between populations results in
Options:
A. a decrease in genetic diversity in the metapopulation
B. a decrease in the rate of beneficial mutations in the metapopulation
C. an increase in genetic homogeneity in the metapopulation
D. a decrease in the likelihood of speciation
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: D. a decrease in the likelihood of speciation

C. 3.33 grams
D. 1.00 grams
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
In his special theory of relativity, Einstein stated that the laws of physics are
Options:
A. the same only in non-accelerating frames of reference.
B. the same in all uniformly moving frames of reference.
C. different in different situations.
D. the same in all frames of reference.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: B. the same in all uniformly moving frames of reference.

When an increase in speed doubles the kinetic energy of a moving body its momentum
Options:
A. more than doubles
B. quadruples
C. increases but less than doubles
D. depends on the mass of the object

per_item.instr:

C. one-fourth as strong
D. inversely proportional to the distance
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
A simple and correct way to comprehend satellites orbiting Earth is to view them as
Options:
A. being held up by the magnetic field of the Earth.
B. beyond the main pull of Earth gravity.
C. having sufficient tangential velocities to fall around rather than into Earth.
D. being pushed by solar winds.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: C. having sufficient tangential velocities to fall around rather than into Earth.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Taphonomy is:
Options:
A. the study of how materials ended up in a particular place.
B. the study of plant and animal life from a particular period in history.
C. a systematic classification based on similarities and differences.
D. a study of the spatial relationships among artifacts, ecofacts, and features.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: D. a study of the spatial relationships among artifacts, ecofacts, and features.

Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Nagel thinks that the core of the absolutist position is that
Options:
A. human persons have a right to life.
B. the ends justify the means.
C. the hostility should be directed at its true object.
D. each person should pursue his or her own happiness.
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: D. each person should pursue his or her own happiness.

per_item.instr:
Listen to the audio and answer the multiple-choice question.
Which is correct about the calcium atom?
Options:
A. All calcium atoms have a mass of 20.078 u
B. It contains 20 protons and 20 electrons
C. All atoms of calcium have 20 protons and 40 neutrons
D. All atoms of calcium have a mass of 40.078 u
Answer with only the option letter and text, e.g. 'A. ...'.
per_item.reply: A. All calcium atoms have a mass of 20.078 u.
